dialogs.content: What is the difference between the lungs of control wild-type mice and IL-17R KO mice at 18 hours after infection?

dialogs.thought: Check for panel labels and timepoints to ensure the correct comparison (18 hours) and groups (Control vs IL-17R-/-).
Orient to histology layout and identify the 18-hour row for both genotypes.

content.content: Composite H&E-stained lung histology figure with two columns (Control on left, IL-17R-/- on right) and three timepoints (0, 18, 24 hours). The 18-hour row shows relatively mild inflammatory cells and edema in control, while IL-17R-/- shows denser inflammatory cell infiltrates around vessels/airways and within alveolar spaces with edema.

arguments.attribute: inflammatory infiltrate and edema at 18 hours in control (left middle panel)
inflammatory infiltrate and edema at 18 hours in IL-17R-/- (right middle panel)

dialogs.thought: Verify the key histopathologic differences at the 18-hour timepoint between control and IL-17R-/- panels.

content.content: Left middle (control, 18h): mostly open alveolar spaces with mild perivascular/peribronchiolar neutrophilic infiltrate; edema present but not extensive.
Right middle (IL-17R-/-, 18h): prominent neutrophils around vessels and bronchioles with extension into alveolar spaces; more conspicuous diffuse perivascular edema and thicker septa.

dialogs.content: At 18 hours after infection, the lungs of control wild-type mice show minimal perivascular infiltration of neutrophils and moderate edema. In contrast, the lungs of IL-17R KO mice exhibit perivascular, peribronchiolar, and intraalveolar neutrophils, along with moderate diffuse perivascular edema. This suggests that the IL-17R KO mice have a more pronounced inflammatory response and edema compared to the control wild-type mice at this time point.
